So what happened? How did Deloitte get awarded this contract? Why didn't they deliver what was needed? And how many more people are going to die because of it?

Flash forward to now: Vaccines have been through trials and are ready to be put into people's arms. Time for VAMS to shine... except it... it doesn't really work. Listen to Maine's CDC director @nirav_mainecdc describe the system to @mainepublic https://t.co/YnbtAae3cA

In June of 2020 government contractor Deloitte held a large conference at Trump's DC hotel, spending at least $300k (according to the NYT) 

November 2020: they were awarded a $32M contract by the CDC to build a Vaccine Administration Management System https://t.co/q802PFA36j

the fact we were unable to build a successful federal website for vaccination distribution and registration when our main export is well-designed software is the loudest quiet scandal we are living  through right now
tale of the pandemic: a tech industry reluctant to lend help and a government reluctant to accept it even if it were offered
